title: Advanced inversion
explanation:
Advanced inversion

At C1 level, inversion appears after more complex triggers: So + adjective ... that, Such ... that, and conditional Should/Were/Had without if.

  • So complex was the problem that experts were called in.
  • Such was the demand that the website crashed.
  • Should you require assistance, please contact us.

questions.text: [*So intense was|Was so intense] the debate that no agreement was reached.
[*Such was|Was such] the public reaction that the minister resigned.
[*Should|If should] you change your mind, let us know immediately.
[*Were|If were] the proposal accepted, funding would be released.
[*Had|If had] we known about the delay, we would have rebooked.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: So convincing ____ the evidence that the jury decided quickly. | *was | is | were
____ you need further clarification, do not hesitate to ask. | *Should | If | When
Such ____ the scale of the disaster that aid arrived from abroad. | *was | were | is
____ I in your position, I would seek independent advice. | *Were | Was | Am
[*So rapidly did|Did so rapidly] prices rise that many tenants protested.
[*Had they not intervened|If they had not intervened] earlier, losses would have been greater.
[*Little did|Did little] anyone suspect the quiet colleague was the author.
[*Such is|Is such] the influence of social media on public opinion today.
[*Were it not for|If it was not for] her research, the project would have failed.
